Figure 2. The FedSDR Framework. Our paradigm mitigates statistical heterogeneity through a three-stage refinery and rectification pipeline. Module 1 (Data Refinery): As shown in Figure 1a, heterogeneous correct raw data is projected into a unified “ModelUnderstanding Space” via self-distillation to generate aligned rewritten data. Module 2 (Dual-Stream Rectification): We adopt an alternating optimization strategy where LoRA-S first absorbs distributional noise and stylistic biases as a functional scaffold, while LoRA-R is subsequently trained on raw data to ensure factual correctness. Module 3 (Selective Aggregation): To avoid the “Rewrite Paradox,” clients only upload the Rectification Adapter (Θr). Since stylistic noise and hallucinations are absorbed locally by LoRA-S, this filtering ensures a globally aligned yet faithful consensus.
Table 9. Taxonomy and Comparison of Federated Learning Frameworks.

Figure 3. The Rewrite Paradox. (a) Distilled data introduces factual hallucinations. (b) Re-written responses become significantly longer, leading to redundancy. (c) The model reinforces its own stylistic biases, doubling the frequency of filler words.
